rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the target component or implementation scope, following AADL structural rules
Preserve existing AADL architecture when inserting annex blocks
Use visible identifiers from the target component or implementation when available
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless properly represented through variables or outputs
generic_rules: Insert annex blocks at the appropriate scope level for the target component or implementation
Preserve the existing AADL architecture when adding annex blocks
Use visible identifiers from the target component or implementation when available
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless properly represented through variables or outputs
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are the normal location for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Component implementation annexes are used for implementation-level bindings such as assignments when they are valid in that scope
anti_patterns: Do not use AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
Do not place assumptions or guarantees that refer to component ports in invalid scopes
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless properly represented through variables or outputs
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax for assume, guarantee, assign, and validator diagnostics
